title: Retrieve broadly, then rerank precisely
description: Cast a wide first-stage retrieval net, then apply a precise reranker to order the small top-k the model actually sees.
body: Use when first-stage retrieval alone gives noisy top results. Do not feed raw vector-similarity top-k straight to the model, and avoid reranking so few candidates that the relevant passage never enters the pool. Actions: retrieve a larger candidate set for recall, rerank with a cross-encoder or scoring model for precision, and pass only the reranked top-k into context. Validation signals include a test that reranking raises top-k relevance over raw retrieval on a labeled set. Risk boundary: if the relevant passage is lost before rerank, the answer cannot be grounded; measure recall at the first stage, not just final precision.
